citations.narrative:
During the winter months, the Zebra are pulled off exhibit and housed in stalls in a large barn that is not temperature
regulated. On January 6, 2026, the new AV arrived for an interview to find an approximate one month old zebra foal down
in an office area and according to the vet, was “ice cold” with "no readable temperature". There was no way to adequately
warm the foal and it died that night of septicemia. According to the vet, the cold temperatures impaired the foals ability to
recover. The daily average temperatures according to “Accu Weather” the week prior to the death were low 20’s F during
the day and in the teens during the overnight hours. Temperatures for zebra’s in the winter need to be sufficiently
regulated to protect the animals, especially young animals from extreme temperatures that would cause harm or
discomfort.
Correct by: Feb 28, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(e)  Critical
   
Handling of animals. 
During the winter months, the Zebra are pulled off exhibit and housed in stalls in a large barn that is not temperature 
regulated. On January 6, 2026, the new AV arrived for an interview to find an approximate one month old zebra foal down 
in an office area and according to the vet, was “ice cold” with "no readable temperature". There was no way to adequately 
warm the foal and it died that night of septicemia. According to the vet, the cold temperatures impaired the foals ability to 
recover. The daily average temperatures according to “Accu Weather” the week prior to the death were low 20’s F during 
the day and in the teens during the overnight hours. Temperatures for zebra’s in the winter need to be sufficiently 
regulated to protect the animals, especially young animals from extreme temperatures that would cause harm or 
discomfort. 
 
Correct by: Feb 28, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
 n
